title: Astronomical object manifest
$comment: An 'object' is a physical transient event — for now, a Type Ia supernova — identified by a repo-internal slug ID and described by coordinates, redshift, classification, and cross-catalog alias records. Aliases carry match_type evidence (how we know two catalog IDs refer to the same event) rather than asserting matches silently. See ADR-0002.

id.description: Repo-internal slug identifier matching the manifest filename stem. Derived from the well-known historical name (e.g. sn-2011fe). Not the TNS name — see tns_name.

name.description: Human-readable name as it appears in the literature (e.g. 'SN 2011fe').

domain.description: Astrophysical domain tags. Must use values from the authoritative enum.

tns_name.description: TNS AT/SN designation if one exists. Null for pre-2016 objects where TNS coverage is absent or retroactive and incomplete. This is not the canonical ID — the repo-internal slug is.

coordinates.description: Working coordinates for this object. Phase 1 records one set of coordinates with a named source. Disagreements across catalogs are a Phase 3 concern.

ra.description: Right ascension in decimal degrees, J2000.

source.description: Which data source provided these coordinates. Use a catalog manifest ID where available, or 'literature' for pre-TNS objects sourced from published discovery reports.

redshift.description: Working redshift. Phase 1 records one value with source type and catalog. Multiple reported redshifts for the same object are a Phase 3 concern.

source_type.description: How the redshift was measured. 'literature' for values adopted from a published compilation without a clear original measurement type.

catalog.description: Catalog manifest ID or 'literature' for the source of this redshift measurement.

host_galaxy.description: Host galaxy association. Optional in schema; expected for all fixture-set objects.

name.description: Common name or designation of the host galaxy.

catalog.description: Catalog manifest ID or 'literature' for the source of this association.

type.$comment: Extend this enum as new domains are added to the registry.

subtype.description: Subtype within the class (e.g. 'normal', '91bg-like', '91T-like', 'broad-lined'). Free string; the vocabulary is still evolving in the literature.

catalog.description: Catalog manifest ID or 'literature' for the source of this classification.

first_observed.description: ISO 8601 date of first reported detection. Optional; may be approximate for historical objects.

aliases.description: Cross-catalog ID records. Each entry is evidence that a catalog's internal identifier refers to this object. match_type records how the correspondence was established.

id.description: The identifier for this object as it appears in the named catalog.

catalog.description: Catalog manifest ID. Must resolve to a file in observables/*/catalogs/.

match_type.description: How the correspondence was established. 'manual' requires a notes entry explaining the basis.

notes.description: Required when match_type is 'manual'. Explain the basis for asserting this correspondence.
